content.full_text:
April 17, 1990

Alice Hughes
FPPC
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Hughes,

I am writing to confirm the advice you provided in our telephone conversation of April 16, 1990, regarding an event that the NWPC of Santa Clara County will be holding on May 4, 1990.

The facts are as follows: we had planned a Spaghetti Dinner to raise money for our NWPC-PAC on the date in question, and sent early notification to labor unions and certain elected officials and community activists who were going to act as servers. Several responded with contributions. Before we mailed the actual invitations, the event was changed to a reception, to raise money for the same NWPC-PAC, on the same date. It is my understanding from our conversation that if we send a letter to the organizations and people who have already sent in money for the spaghetti dinner, telling them that the event has been changed, and that they can have their checks back if they notify us, we will be in compliance with applicable California campaign laws. If they want their spaghetti dinner checks to pay for their attendance at the reception, they do not have to issue a new check.

Please confirm this advice in writing. We really want to be in compliance. Thank you very much.

Sincerely,

Mary Ruth Gross
Past President

cc: Anhela Oropeza
     Eleanor Pett
